pg67-p1-s1: Though we did not like their symptoms and the way these disturbed us, they, like ourselves, were sick too.
pg67-p1-s2:
We asked God
to help us show them the same tolerance, pity, and patience that we would cheerfully grant a sick friend.
pg67-p1-s3:
When a person
offended we said to ourselves, "This is a sick man.
pg67-p1-s4: How can I be helpful to him?
pg67-p1-s5: God save me from being angry.
pg67-p1:
Though we did not like their symptoms and the way these disturbed us, they, like ourselves, were sick too. We asked God
to help us show them the same tolerance, pity, and patience that we would cheerfully grant a sick friend. When a person
offended we said to ourselves, "This is a sick man. How can I be helpful to him? God save me from being angry. Thy will
be done."
pg67-p2-s2: We wouldn't treat sick people that way.
pg67-p2-s3:
If we do, we destroy our chance of being
helpful.
pg67-p2-s4:
We cannot be helpful to all people, but at least God will show us how to take a kindly and tolerant view of
each and every one.
pg67-p2:
We avoid retaliation or argument. We wouldn't treat sick people that way. If we do, we destroy our chance of being
helpful. We cannot be helpful to all people, but at least God will show us how to take a kindly and tolerant view of
each and every one.
pg67-p3-s2:
Putting out of our minds the wrongs others had done, we resolutely looked for our own
mistakes.
pg67-p3-s3: Where had we been selfish, dishonest, self-seeking and frightened?
pg67-p3-s4:
Though a situation had not been entirely
our fault, we tried to disregard the other person involved entirely.
pg67-p3-s6:
The inventory was ours, not
the other man's.
pg67-p3-s7: When we saw our faults we listed them.
pg67-p3-s8: We placed them before us in black and white.
pg67-p3-s9:
We admitted our
wrongs honestly and were willing to set these matters straight.
pg67-p3:
Referring to our list again. Putting out of our minds the wrongs others had done, we resolutely looked for our own
mistakes. Where had we been selfish, dishonest, self-seeking and frightened? Though a situation had not been entirely
our fault, we tried to disregard the other person involved entirely. Where were we to blame? The inventory was ours, not
the other man's. When we saw our faults we listed them. We placed them before us in black and white. We admitted our
wrongs honestly and were willing to set these matters straight.
pg67-p4-s1:
Notice that the word "fear" is bracketed alongside the difficulties with Mr. Brown, Mrs. Jones, the employer, and the
wife.
pg67-p4-s2: This short word somehow touches about every aspect of our lives.
pg67-p4-s3:
It was an evil and corroding thread; the fabric
of our existence was shot through with it.
pg67-p4-s4:
It set in motion trains of circumstances which brought us misfortune we felt
we didn't deserve.
pg67-p4-s5: But did not we, ourselves, set the ball rolling?
pg67-p4:
Notice that the word "fear" is bracketed alongside the difficulties with Mr. Brown, Mrs. Jones, the employer, and the
wife. This short word somehow touches about every aspect of our lives. It was an evil and corroding thread; the fabric
of our existence was shot through with it. It set in motion trains of circumstances which brought us misfortune we felt
we didn't deserve. But did not we, ourselves, set the ball rolling? Sometimes
